essential_question: Can you show, independently, everything this year built?
objective: Complete the cumulative Math-U-See Final Exam from the Test Booklet, independently and under real test conditions, with no help mid-test.

content.heading: Not a new lesson — the real thing
bullets: Today's work is the MUS Final Exam, from the Test Booklet, cumulative across all 30 lessons.
It's completed independently: no notes, no help, no answer key, no AI tool while it's in progress.
If you truly get stuck, skip the problem and flag it — we debrief it next session, not now.

content.heading: Independent work time
content.note: Take the MUS Final Exam now, straight through, from the Test Booklet. This block may run longer than 32 minutes — that's fine; work at a steady, careful pace rather than watching the clock. When you finish, come back here for the exit questions.

content.preview: Next session: debrief the hard parts and celebrate the year.

success_criteria.text: I can complete the Final Exam independently, following the materials and no-help rules exactly

questions.prompt: Confirm you have your Test Booklet, a pencil, a ruler, and a protractor, and that you have a quiet block of uninterrupted time with no notes or help nearby.
Which of these is allowed during the Final Exam?
questions.explain: The Final Exam is completed independently — no hints, answer keys, or AI tools — but a short break for water is fine.

questions.prompt: If you get stuck on a problem mid-exam, what's the rule?
questions.answer: Skip it and flag it for the debrief next session — don't look it up or ask for help.
questions.explain: Matches the course's assessment-integrity policy for the Final Exam.

questions.prompt: After finishing: which section(s) felt hardest? You'll debrief them next session.
